## [06:03 - 09:04]

The one place that a coach can really take things the wrong direction is when a coach becomes overly controlling. And a coach is charged with the client's goals of improved performance. It's a co-created space and you are in charge of helping your client with expanding their clarity, helping them with the commitment to their goal, helping them understand what their competency is needed for their next achievement and helping them to navigate complex environments. But sometimes, sometimes the coach can get confused and start moving more into a puppeteer. So to help you understand this, let's head over to the whiteboard and I'm going to teach you the concept of the box. So this is a pretty simple idea, but it makes all the difference. So here I am, the coach and here is the client and the box represents what the client can see in terms of their perspective, their values, their life experience. And a mistake that amateur coaches often make is they think that in order for their client to be successful, the coach has to go ahead and hop into the box with them. And they do this a lot of ways. They say, okay, well, here's my personal cell phone number. Make sure you call me morning, noon and night. Make sure you email me about absolutely everything that's going on in your life. And also make sure that you blame me for everything. So where this is pretty horrible is it kind of turns this client into being very, very dependent on the coach as if the coach is personally responsible for every aspect of the client's life. That's not what coaching is. That's not what coaching ever should be. And to help free you of this, I want you to remember that coaching is actually a relationship. The fact is you coach actually are also in a box. You're both in a box. You have views, you have perspectives, you have core values, you have beliefs, you have experiences and they're valuable. They're amazing, but they're yours, just like the client has theirs. And coaching ultimately is a relationship. And you're having conversations with each other that's sharing perspective across a boundary of that relationship. A premium coaching relationship is a professional relationship. And it's one where you as the coach are a facilitator of that professional relationship. So it's very important that you are really, really clear about what is the goal of that
